qa_pairs.question: What is the process for accessing Windows files from the Linux command line when using Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL)?
qa_pairs.answer: To access Windows files from the Linux command line in WSL, first find the full path of the file or folder on Windows. Then, note the drive letter (e.g., C:\), replace it with /mnt/{letter}/ (e.g., /mnt/c/), and transform all backslashes (\) to forward slashes (/). For example, C:\Users\user1\Documents\File1.txt becomes /mnt/c/Users/user1/Documents/File1.txt.

qa_pairs.question: How is a Windows file path like `C:\Users\user1\Documents\File1.txt` translated for access from the Linux command line in Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL)?
qa_pairs.answer: When accessing from the Linux command line in Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), the Windows file path `C:\Users\user1\Documents\File1.txt` is translated to `/mnt/c/Users/user1/Documents/File1.txt`. The drive letter `C:` is replaced with `/mnt/c/`, and backslashes are converted to forward slashes.
